claim: Phi-4 models add function calling to Phi-4-mini (3.8B), enabling local function-calling agents and multi-tool decision-making without cloud inference.
actionable: For lightweight local agents with tool calling, deploy Phi-4-mini (3.8B) via Ollama or ONNX runtime on your machine instead of relying on cloud APIs.

claim: Phi-4 multimodal (5.6B) is a fully multimodal model with both vision encoder (SigLip) and audio encoder (trained on 2M hours of speech), allowing interleaved image/text/audio processing in a single inference.
actionable: For multimodal tasks (OCR, audio transcription, image QA), consider Phi-4 multimodal as a 5.6B local option that handles all three modalities at once.

claim: Phi-4 multimodal shows very high accuracy on OCR (transcribing text from screenshots) and audio transcription, even surpassing Whisper in some cases, despite being smaller.
actionable: For OCR and audio transcription in local workflows, test Phi-4 multimodal against Whisper; it may offer better accuracy in your use case.

claim: Phi models are built on synthetic data and optimized for math and coding through careful synthetic dataset curation, evolving with each version to find what works.
actionable: Expect Phi-4 variants to excel at math and code reasoning due to synthetic training; use them for coding/math tasks where curated data helps.

claim: Phi-4 models come with ONNX quantized versions for deployment on devices like Raspberry Pi and mobile phones, making edge deployment practical.
actionable: For edge AI (Raspberry Pi, phones, IoT), use ONNX quantized Phi-4 variants instead of larger models or cloud APIs.

claim: Phi-4 was trained on 5 trillion tokens (less than newer models at 12-15T), but relies heavily on synthetic data quality to compensate for lower scale.
actionable: Understand that Phi-4's strength comes from synthetic data design, not just raw training tokens. It shows smaller isn't always worse if data is well-curated.
